channel.title: Blog.jetbrains.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Blog.jetbrains.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Using Spring Data JPA with Kotlin
item.description: Spring Data JPA is based on the Jakarta Persistence specification and was originally designed for Java. That often raises the question of whether it is a good fit for Kotlin projects. The short answer is yes! You can use Spring Data JPA with Kotlin without any issues and enjoy Kotlin’s compact syntax and language features, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/using-spring-data-jpa-with-kotlin/

item.title: ReSharper C++ 2026.1: Better performance, improved Unreal Engine workflows, and language support updates
item.description: ReSharper C++ 2026.1 is here, bringing performance improvements, expanded language support, and better tooling for Unreal Engine development. This release focuses on improved performance in large C++ codebases, enhancements in coding assistance and code analysis, and continuing support for modern C and C++ standards. If you’d like to explore the full set of updates, take […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/rscpp/2026/03/30/resharper-cpp-2026-1-released/

item.title: ReSharper 2026.1: Built-in Performance Monitoring, Expansion to VS Code, and Faster Everyday Workflows
item.description: ReSharper 2026.1 is here, bringing improvements that focus on how you actually work: understanding runtime behavior, writing safer C#, and keeping Visual Studio responsive. This release introduces a new approach to performance monitoring, takes ReSharper beyond Visual Studio, and delivers a range of updates to code analysis, C++ support, and overall IDE responsiveness. A new […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/dotnet/2026/03/30/resharper-2026-1-released/

item.title: Rider 2026.1: More AI Choice, Stronger .NET Tooling, and Expanded Game Dev Support
item.description: Rider 2026.1 is here, bringing improvements across the areas that matter most: .NET development, game development workflows, and the overall IDE experience. Key updates include the ability to run file-based C# programs, inspect .NET disassemblies, and manage NuGet packages more easily. On the game development side, Rider handles Unreal Engine projects better than ever, mobile […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/dotnet/2026/03/30/rider-2026-1-released/

item.title: JetBrains Academy – March Digest
item.description: Hey! March is moving fast, but we’ve already packed this digest with opportunities to learn, build, and plan your next step in tech. Inside: free student tools, a summer coding camp, a scholarship-supported bachelor’s program, and inspiring stories from our community. Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/education/2026/03/30/jetbrains-academy-march-2026/

item.title: DataGrip 2026.1: Redesigned Query Files, Data Source Templates in Your JetBrains Account, AI Agents in the AI Chat, Explain Plan Flow Enhancements, and More!
item.description: DataGrip 2026.1, the first major update of the year, is here! Let’s take a look at what’s inside. Download DataGrip 2026.1 Query files and consoles In this release, we are redesigning the flow for working with query files side by side with query consoles. This way, you can use either or both of them, depending […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/datagrip/2026/03/26/datagrip-2026-1-redesigned-query-files-data-source-templates-in-your-jetbrains-account-ai-agents-in-the-ai-chat-explain-plan-flow-enhancements-and-more/

item.title: Comparative Analysis of Development Cycle Speed in Java and Kotlin Based on IDE Telemetry Data
item.description: Introduction Does the choice of programming language affect how fast developers deliver code? This question matters for engineering teams evaluating technology stacks, yet it is notoriously hard to answer. Self-reported surveys suffer from recall bias, lines-of-code comparisons conflate conciseness with productivity, and controlled experiments rarely scale beyond a handful of participants. In 2024, Meta introduced […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/research/2026/03/comparative-analysis-of-development-cycle-speed-in-java-and-kotlin/

item.title: PhpStorm 2026.1 is Now Out
item.description: Welcome to PhpStorm 2026.1! This release brings new PhpStorm MCP tools, new third-party agents inside your IDE, support for Git worktrees, and lots of other productivity-enhancing features for PHP and Laravel developers. Download PhpStorm 2026.1 PhpStorm MCP tools In PhpStorm 2025.2, we added an integrated MCP server for third-party coding agents like Claude Code, Windsurf, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/phpstorm/2026/03/phpstorm-2026-1-is-now-out/

item.title: AI-Assisted Java Application Development with Agent Skills
item.description: Agent-assisted development is quickly becoming a common mode of software development. New techniques are emerging to help LLMs generate code that matches your preferences and standards. One common approach is to create an AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or GEMINI.md file with project details, build instructions, and coding guidelines. The AI agent loads this file into context on […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/ai-assisted-java-application-development-with-agent-skills/

item.title: RubyMine 2026.1: AI Chat Upgrades, New Code Insight, Stable Remote Development, and More
item.description: RubyMine 2026.1 is here! This release brings a range of improvements aimed at making Ruby and Rails development faster and more enjoyable. You can get the new build from our website or via the free Toolbox App. Let’s take a look at the highlights of this release. AI RubyMine continues to evolve as an open platform that lets […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/ruby/2026/03/rubymine-2026-1-ai-chat-upgrades-new-code-insight-stable-remote-development-and-more/

item.title: GoLand 2026.1 Is Released
item.description: GoLand 2026.1 helps you keep your Go code modern and your workflow efficient. This release introduces guided syntax updates for Go 1.26, making it easier to adopt new language improvements across your entire codebase. It also expands AI capabilities with support for additional agents, and brings several productivity improvements, including Git worktrees support, and a […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/go/2026/03/26/goland-2026-1-is-released/

item.title: CLion 2026.1 Is Here
item.description: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Other Agents in the AI Chat, Support for Custom Project Formats, DAP Debugging via TCP, and More CLion 2026.1 focuses on stability and improving the existing functionality, but that didn’t stop us from shipping some exciting new features. Most notably, you can now use more agents directly in the AI chat, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/clion/2026/03/2026-1-release/

item.title: Best CI Tools for 2026: What the Data Actually Shows
item.description: Continuous integration (CI) and continuous delivery or deployment (CD) are core DevOps practices that help teams improve code quality by giving fast, reliable feedback on every code change. With a well-established CI/CD process in place, software development teams can release more frequently, deliver value to users sooner, and learn faster from their feedback.CI/CD is now […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/teamcity/2026/03/best-ci-tools/

item.title: Expanding Our Core Web Development Support in PyCharm 2026.1
item.description: With PyCharm 2026.1, our core IDE experience continues to evolve as we’re bringing a broader set of professional-grade web tools to all users for free. Everyone, from beginners to backend-first developers, is getting access to a substantial set of JavaScript, TypeScript, and CSS features that were previously only available with a Pro subscription. React, JavaScript, […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/pycharm/2026/03/expanding-our-core-web-development-support-in-pycharm-2026-1/

item.title: WebStorm 2026.1: Service-powered TypeScript Engine, Junie, Claude Agent, and Codex in the AI chat, Framework Updates, and More
item.description: WebStorm 2026.1 is now available! This release focuses on the everyday web development workflows where IDE support matters most, helping you stay productive in large TypeScript projects, making it easy to keep up with frameworks that evolve quickly, and bringing AI tools into the IDE so you don’t have to switch contexts. The highlights of […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/webstorm/2026/03/webstorm-2026-1/

item.title: IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 Is Out!
item.description: IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 is here, and it comes packed with an array of new features and enhancements to elevate your coding experience! You can download this latest release from our website or update to it directly from inside the IDE, via the free Toolbox App, or using snap packages for Ubuntu. As always, all new features are brought […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/intellij-idea-2026-1/

item.title: What’s fixed in IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1
item.description: Welcome to the overview of fixes and improvements in IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1. In this release, we have resolved over 1,000 bugs and usability issues, including 334 reported by users. Below are the most impactful changes that will help you work with greater confidence every day. Performance We continue to prioritize reliability, working to improve application […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/idea/2026/03/whats-fixed-intellij-idea-2026-1/

item.title: Introducing JetBrains Central: An Open System for Agentic Software Development
item.description: AI is beginning to change how software is produced. Instead of just assisting developers inside the editor, AI agents now investigate issues, generate code, run tests, and execute multi-step workflows. As this work scales, software development extends beyond individual tools or sessions. It becomes a distributed system of agents, environments, and workflows that operate across […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/blog/2026/03/24/introducing-jetbrains-central-an-open-system-for-agentic-software-development/

item.title: OpenAI Acquires Astral: What It Means for PyCharm Users
item.description: On March 19, OpenAI announced that it would acquire Astral, the company behind uv, Ruff, and ty. The Astral team, led by founder Charlie Marsh, will join OpenAI’s Codex team. The deal is subject to regulatory approval. First and foremost: congratulations to Charlie Marsh and the entire Astral team. They shipped some of the most […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/pycharm/2026/03/openai-acquires-astral-what-it-means-for-pycharm-users/

item.title: KotlinConf’26 Speakers: In Conversation with Josh Long
item.description: “There’s never been a better time to be a JVM or Spring developer.” The Spring ecosystem has evolved dramatically over the past decade, from traditional enterprise applications to microservices, distributed systems, and now AI-powered services. Few people have witnessed that evolution as closely as Josh Long, who has served as Spring’s first Developer Advocate since […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/kotlin/2026/03/kotlinconf-26-speakers-in-conversation-with-josh-long/

item.title: The New Role of Data Teams in the Agentic Analytics Era
item.description: Last week, in the first part of this series, we explained why two analysts can produce two different answers from the same data, and why that problem gets worse with AI agents. Without a shared semantic layer defining metrics and business logic, AI will generate answers faster, but not more reliably. Today we’ll focus on […] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/databao/2026/03/new-role-of-data-teams/
